game_name: Castle of Illusion Starring Mickey Mouse

design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate side-scrolling levels, defeat enemies via bounce-jumping and projectile collection, defeat Master of Illusion bosses to collect Rainbow Gems, progress to final witch confrontation
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Platforming precision combined with enemy avoidance; resource management through health pickups and extra lives
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Rescue Minnie Mouse as Mickey by mastering a magical castle filled with illusion-themed worlds and whimsical enemies
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Emphasizes accessibility across skill levels with explicit easy mode (truncated first three levels, no bosses) while maintaining challenge for experienced players; bounce-jump as unified attack-and-mobility mechanic shows design conviction around Mickey's silhouette and physics

difficulty.lives_system: Extra tries available as collectible items throughout levels
difficulty.checkpointing: Per-level progression with boss encounters at level end (except final two levels which award gems mid-level)
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Easy mode option available from start, allowing players to experience story with reduced mechanical complexity and no boss fights

progression.upgrade_system: Collectible projectiles (apples, marbles) for temporary combat options; bonus point items and health restoration pickups
progression.unlock_mechanism: Seven Gems of the Rainbow collected sequentially from five main levels; all gems required to unlock route to final Mizrabel boss fight
replayability_hooks: Easy mode vs. normal mode distinction
Bonus point collectibles encourage thorough exploration
Different enemy and level layouts in 8-bit Master System/Game Gear versions

world_building_techniques: Thematic level design (Enchanted Forest, Toyland, The Storm, Dessert Factory, The Library, The Castle)
Enemy archetypes tied to world theme (enchanted mushrooms in forest, toy soldiers in toyland, etc.)
Boss encounters as narrative progression markers (Masters of Illusion as Mizrabel's henchmen)
Climactic final boss transformation (giant, youthful Mizrabel) as story payoff

boss_design.philosophy: Each level features a Master of Illusion boss guarding a Rainbow Gem; boss defeats serve dual narrative and progression purpose; final encounter against Mizrabel herself represents story climax
notable_bosses: Masters of Illusion (level-specific guardians, one per level)
Mizrabel (final boss, giant youthful witch form)

faction_indicators: Mizrabel as central antagonist controlling Masters of Illusion
Castle kingdom ruler as ally providing quest direction
danger_signals: Mizrabel's youth-stealing intention toward Minnie creates urgency
Enchanted armies (mushrooms, clowns, knights) as manifestation of witch's control
environmental_storytelling: Illusion-themed level design establishes magical, corrupted castle aesthetic
Enemy types reflect level themes, reinforcing world cohesion
Gem collection provides visible progress toward ultimate goal

sources_used: Wikipedia - Castle of Illusion Starring Mickey Mouse
